response.justification: While an accomplished software engineer with 20+ years of experience, Maria Rodriguez lacks the core technical requirements for this C++ developer role. Her C++ experience is minimal (6 months at National Instruments in 2014-2015) and her recent decade-long career has been exclusively in web and backend development using JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang. She has no background in industrial automation, manufacturing, IoT, or industrial cybersecurity standards (IEC 62443), which are central to this position. The alignment gap on both the primary language and domain expertise makes this a poor fit.
strengths: 20+ years of software development experience with proven architectural and systems-thinking capabilities across web, mobile, and embedded contexts
Demonstrated expertise in high-performance, low-latency systems (Dunnhumby) and event-driven architectures using async I/O and caching
Strong mentoring track record and teaching background (workshops, courses, open-source contributions) indicating ability to elevate team standards
concerns: C++ expertise is minimal and dated—only 6 months at National Instruments (2014-2015); entire recent 10-year career is JavaScript/TypeScript/DLang, not systems or embedded C++ development
No background in industrial automation, manufacturing, IoT, or industrial cybersecurity; no exposure to IEC 62443, SDL practices, or specific industrial protocols (Modbus, IEC 61850, OPC UA)
Computer Science degree rather than required B.E./B.Tech/M.E./M.Tech in engineering; no MQTT, multi-threaded systems, or message-broker experience in C++ context
